pi_items.source: Verified by reading post_task.sh line 10: GPKG_OUT="/sdcard/wetland_delineation_verification_result.gpkg". Live-tested on device — post_task.sh produced this exact file at this path.
Verified by querying gpkg_contents on device: sqlite3 shows all 3 table_name entries matching. Live screenshot confirms all 3 layers visible in QField layers panel.

The task describes a USACE Section 404 wetland delineation field verification workflow. Confirmed via USACE Wetlands Delineation Manual (1987) and NRCS Field Indicators of Hydric Soils. Hydric indicators in the data (depleted_matrix=F3, hydrogen_sulfide=A4, loamy_gleyed_matrix=F2, redox_concentrations=F5, depleted_below=A11) are all real NRCS-approved field indicators per Version 8.2/9.0.

Confirmed via O*NET (onetonline.org/link/summary/19-4042.00) and BLS OES (bls.gov/oes/2023/may/oes194042.htm). SOC 19-4042.00 = Environmental Science and Protection Technicians, Including Health. These professionals perform field tests to monitor the environment and investigate sources of pollution, which aligns with wetland delineation verification.

pi_items.metadata_value: USACE wetland delineation protocols, Upper Mississippi River floodplain, La Crosse WI
pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic data based on USACE wetland delineation protocols. Location is the real Upper Mississippi River floodplain near La Crosse, WI (lat 43.74-43.91, lon -91.31 to -91.17). Domain terminology (Cowardin codes, Munsell colors, hydric indicators, vegetation species) is realistic and follows USACE/NRCS/NWI standards, but the specific wetland polygons, soil borings, and attribute values are synthetically generated.
pi_items.source: Confirmed by reading generate_wetland_delineation_gpkg.py — data is hardcoded in the script, not fetched from any API. Cowardin codes (PEM1C, PFO1A, PSS1C, PEM1F, L2UBH, PFO1C) are valid NWI codes per fws.gov/program/national-wetlands-inventory/classification-codes. Munsell colors (10YR 4/1, GLEY1 4/10Y, etc.) follow real Munsell notation. Plant species (Typha latifolia, Carex stricta, etc.) are real wetland indicator species found in the Upper Mississippi region.

privileged_info_summary: The GeoPackage wetland_delineation_verification.gpkg contains 3 layers: wetland_boundaries (6 polygons), soil_borings (14 points), and verification_results (empty). The agent must cross-reference soil_borings with wetland_boundaries using the wetland_id attribute and the rule that a wetland is CONFIRMED if at least 2 borings have is_wetland_positive=1. The ground truth classifications are: WL-001=CONFIRMED (borings SB-001 positive, SB-002 positive, 2/2), WL-002=REJECTED (borings SB-003 negative, SB-004 negative, SB-005 negative, 0/3), WL-003=CONFIRMED (borings SB-006 positive, SB-007 positive, 2/2), WL-004=REJECTED (borings SB-008 negative, SB-009 positive, SB-010 negative, 1/3), WL-005=CONFIRMED (borings SB-011 positive, SB-012 positive, 2/2), WL-006=REJECTED (borings SB-013 negative, SB-014 negative, 0/2). For all 6 wetlands, field_verified must be set to CONFIRMED or REJECTED, verification_date to '2024-09-15', and delineator to 'Survey Team Alpha'. For the 3 REJECTED wetlands (WL-002, WL-004, WL-006), boundary_accuracy must be set to 'FAILED', and a verification_results point must be created with result_id=<wetland_id>_RESULT, finding='INSUFFICIENT_HYDRIC_INDICATORS', and notes listing the negative boring IDs (WL-002: SB-003, SB-004, SB-005; WL-004: SB-008, SB-010; WL-006: SB-013, SB-014). The primary_reference wetland is WL-005, which has the shallowest average depth_to_water_cm among CONFIRMED wetlands: WL-001 avg=11.5cm (15+8)/2, WL-003 avg=20.0cm (22+18)/2, WL-005 avg=8.5cm (5+12)/2. WL-005's nwi_status must be updated to 'primary_reference'. The map shows the La Crosse, Wisconsin area with 2 prominent red polygons visible at default zoom (WL-004 near Onalaska and WL-005 near Crescent); the agent needs to pan to find all 6 wetlands. Borings are spatially located inside their associated wetland polygons. Attribute forms require scrolling to see all fields — is_wetland_positive is the 8th column in soil_borings, and field_verified is the 6th column in wetland_boundaries.
